title: Week of April 6, 2026
summary: This week brings significant reliability improvements to OpsPal, with automatic cleanup of outdated system files and better error handling during startup. Salesforce users benefit from enhanced Dynamic Forms detection that prevents field conflicts, plus automatic record type assignments that eliminate common setup errors. The platform now automatically installs missing dependencies and provides clearer guidance when manual fixes are needed.

entries.headline: Reduced startup noise from non-critical system messages
Automatic system cleanup removes outdated files and sessions
details: Removes old cache files older than 7 days
Cleans up abandoned sessions after 5 minutes
Alerts you when memory logs get too large

entries.headline: Removes broken automation scripts across all operating systems
details: Works consistently on Windows, Mac, and Linux
Cleans up scripts from any plugin source
Automatically detects and removes non-functional automations

entries.headline: Automatically removes unused plugins when you start OpsPal
Major Salesforce improvements prevent field conflicts and setup errors
details: Detects Dynamic Forms to prevent invisible field additions
Automatically assigns new record types to avoid permission errors
Reduces session interruptions from routing conflicts

entries.headline: Automatically installs missing components and shows clear error messages
details: Installs required dependencies when you start OpsPal
Shows helpful commands when manual fixes are needed
Runs background repairs for common issues

entries.headline: Resolved false error messages about missing packages
